name: Monks In Da High House
description:
Preface:

It happened that I inherited a complete collection of Legends of the Five Rings cards from a friend (including Under Fu Leng's Shadow) published by FFG.

I wasn't into that game when it was released or played, but I now am very drawn to the setting (currently I really enjoy playing RPG in this setting) and the mechanics of this card game.

I decided to build decks for each clan in the archetype I liked the most. I hope to try them out with friends someday, either offline or online in Jigoku.

I've studied a lot of online materials on L5R deckbuilding and have quite a bit of experience myself (MTG, AGoT (CCG and LCG 1.0 and 2.0), and Netrunner LCG), but I'm still open to any suggestions and recommendations regarding deck building.

While building the decks, I tried to adhere to the latest version of the Restricted and Banned Lists.

Deck: Monks In Da High House

Based on what I could find on the internet, most competitive Dragon decks focus on one key character: Togashi Mitsu. In both his earlier and later iterations, Mitsu is capable of winning games singlehandedly through impressive recursion or ring manipulation, along with the support of key events, attachments, and provinces. Beyond this, Dragon dueling decks, which rely on the action abilities of Togashi Yokuni and Niten Master, can also be quite powerful.

As for me, I decided to build a deck with the monk sub-theme and the later iteration of Mitsu.

It just feels good to use all of those Kihō spells one by one pronouncing their names. My kung-fu is stronger than your kung-fu.

Anyway, the Lion splash Dragon deck makes use of the free stand from In Service to My Lord to ensure the second iteration of Togashi Mitsu is present in almost every conflict.

The titular rings of L5R each affect the game in fundamental ways and Togashi Mitsu maximizes these depending on the current board state. Need card advantage? Resolve the Ring of Earth. Need honor against Scorpion? Trigger the Ring of Air. Need a bow or ready in a pinch? The Ring of Water will provide. And, provided it isn't instantly discarded, Way of the Dragon allows this to be done again if needed.

Repeated card use is needed to trigger Togashi Mitsu and such play synergizes well with High House of Light to ensure he sticks around each turn. The ability to continually play cards is granted by free events which allow another further cards to be drawn, such as Hurricane Punch and Iron Foundations Stance. These cards can be tutored with Ancient Master (since Ki Alignment is banned). Finally, each of these plays also charge Togashi Acolyte, ensuring that Void Fist can send home key characters as needed.

Province of the choice for the stronghold is obviously Sacred Sanctuary.
Although the province's strength of 2 is weak, the reaction provides outstanding value, allowing a key character to ready (and thus join as a defender), and remain unbowed at the end of the conflict. This character is also immune to bow effects (although send home and discard effects will still work if High House of Light is not used in a timely fashion).

In practice, Sacred Sanctuary allows a key Monk character to participate in three conflicts every turn. If the Dragon player has the First Player token, they can attack with a key character, and then ready and defend with Sacred Sanctury. The character then remains readied at the end of the conflict, and is thus able to declare as an attacker or defender in a subsequent conflict. 

If the Dragon player does not have the First Player token, they can defend as usual in their first conflict, and then trigger the stronghold for their second defense, thus readying a key character who will subsequently be able to declare as an attacker for the final conflict of the phase. 

When a powerful Monk such as Togashi Mitsu is honored and supported by multiple attachments or events, this can be a real headache for opponents. 

Alternatively, you could switch to a Water role, which also allows you to add Renowned Singer to recur key cards as needed. 
As well as, Slovenly Scavenger can help to offset the extreme card draw, while Indomitable Will, Wildfire Kick, and Venerable Fortunist are also possible inclusions. 

The archetype can be further iterated upon by using Togashi Tadakatsu and the appropriate mantra cards.
